name: Climate Migration System Dynamics
description: This model captures the dynamics driving climate-induced migration, its impacts on host regions, and subsequent social, economic, and environmental feedback.

nodes.label: Climate Change
nodes.desc: Long-term shifts in temperatures and weather patterns, often exacerbated by human activities.
nodes.example: Increase in global average temperatures by 1.2°C since pre-industrial times.

nodes.label: Extreme Weather Events
nodes.desc: Occurrence of unusually severe weather conditions, like hurricanes or droughts.
nodes.example: 2017 Hurricane Maria caused widespread devastation in Puerto Rico.

nodes.label: Livelihood Disruptions
nodes.desc: Impact on local economies and employment due to climate effects, forcing people to move.
nodes.example: Farmers in Bangladesh losing crops to recurrent flooding, leading to loss of income.

nodes.label: Migration Flow
nodes.desc: Movement of people from affected regions to more stable areas, often international.
nodes.example: In 2020, approximately 30 million climate migrants were displaced worldwide.

nodes.label: Host Region Population
nodes.desc: Population size in the areas receiving climate migrants.
nodes.example: The European Union received over 200,000 asylum applications from climate-affected regions in 2019.

nodes.label: Resource Stress
nodes.desc: Increased pressure on local resources like water, housing, and jobs in host regions.
nodes.example: Cities like Dhaka experiencing resource shortages due to an influx of rural climate migrants.

nodes.label: Social Tension
nodes.desc: Strained relationships between new migrants and existing population in host regions.
nodes.example: Rising xenophobia in areas with high migrant populations.

nodes.label: Adaptive Policies
nodes.desc: Government and organizational responses to manage climate migration and integrate migrants.
nodes.example: Development of resettlement schemes for displaced people in Australia.

nodes.label: Economic Growth
nodes.desc: Overall economic development in host regions, influenced by workforce changes.
nodes.example: Migration contributing to labor market expansion in Germany.

nodes.label: Environmental Degradation
nodes.desc: Deterioration of the natural environment due to overuse of resources in host regions.
nodes.example: Increased deforestation in the Amazon due to land-clearance for settlement.

loops.name: Climate Migration Pressure
loops.desc: Climate change causes extreme weather events and livelihood disruptions, increasing migration flow, and thus population pressure in host regions.

loops.name: Resource and Social Stress
loops.desc: As migration flows increase, host region population grows, leading to resource stress and social tension, which can further destabilize conditions.

loops.name: Adaptive Policy Intervention
loops.desc: Increasing social tension prompts adaptive policies, aimed at stabilizing the economic and social impacts of migration.

loops.name: Environmental Feedback
loops.desc: Rising population in host regions leads to environmental degradation, which can contribute back to global climate change.

archetypes.name: Tragedy of the Commons
archetypes.description: Environmental degradation in host regions due to overuse of resources by increased population, feeding back into climate change.

archetypes.name: Limits to Growth
archetypes.description: Resource stress and social tension escalate as population grows, potentially limiting the sustainable capacity of host regions.
